Description: Initialize FastAPI project with standard directory structure (routers / models / schemas / utils). Configure uvicorn as ASGI server. Set up environment variable loading via python-dotenv (.env.dev / .env.prod). Implement /health endpoint. Add basic logging configuration.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] FastAPI app initializes without errors; [ ] Directory structure follows routers/models/schemas/utils convention; [ ] /health returns {status: ok} with 200; [ ] Environment variables loaded from .env file; [ ] uvicorn starts correctly; [ ] Logging outputs to stdout in structured format

Description: Set up Azure SQL database connection using SQLAlchemy with pyodbc driver. Configure connection pool (pool_size, max_overflow, pool_timeout). Implement Azure AD Service Principal authentication for the database connection. Add connection retry logic with exponential backoff. Expose a get_db() dependency for FastAPI route injection.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] SQLAlchemy engine connects to Azure SQL successfully; [ ] Service Principal auth works (no username/password in code); [ ] Connection pool configured with appropriate limits; [ ] Retry logic handles transient connection failures; [ ] get_db() dependency yields session and closes on exit; [ ] Connection string built from env variables only

Description: Implement JWT token validation middleware for Azure AD tokens. Validate token signature, issuer, audience, and expiry. Extract user_id (oid claim) and email (upn or email claim) from validated token. Inject user context into FastAPI request state. Return 401 on missing or invalid token. Exclude /health from auth requirement.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Valid Azure AD JWT passes validation; [ ] Expired or tampered token returns 401; [ ] user_id and email correctly extracted from token claims; [ ] User context available via request.state.user in all protected routes; [ ] /health endpoint bypasses auth; [ ] Token validation uses Azure AD JWKS endpoint (no hardcoded secrets)

Description: Define unified response envelope: {data, error, meta}. Implement global FastAPI exception handlers for HTTPException, SQLAlchemy errors, and unhandled exceptions. All endpoints must return responses conforming to the envelope schema. Add request ID to response headers for traceability.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] All successful responses follow {data: ..., error: null, meta: {request_id, timestamp}}; [ ] All error responses follow {data: null, error: {code, message}, meta: ...}; [ ] HTTPException mapped to appropriate envelope; [ ] SQLAlchemy errors return 500 without leaking stack traces; [ ] Request ID generated per request and returned in X-Request-ID header

Description: Configure CORS middleware with allowed origins whitelist (dev + prod frontend URLs). Write Dockerfile for FastAPI app (python:3.11-slim base, non-root user). Write OpenShift deployment YAML (Deployment, Service, Route). Integrate with Harness CI/CD pipeline for build and deploy to OpenShift namespace.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] CORS allows requests from configured frontend origins only; [ ] Dockerfile builds successfully and runs as non-root user; [ ] OpenShift Deployment YAML applies without errors; [ ] Service and Route expose the BFF correctly; [ ] Harness pipeline builds image and deploys to correct namespace; [ ] Health check passes post-deploy

Description: Define TypeScript interfaces for all BFF request and response shapes. Place in frontend repo at src/types/api.ts. This file is the single source of truth for the API contract — any BFF endpoint change must update this file first. Cover all endpoints: companies, favorites, recent, activity, preferences, insights, metrics, trends, loan portfolio, risk insights, activity feed, follow-up questions, export.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] src/types/api.ts covers request/response types for all 18 BFF endpoints; [ ] Types are exported and imported by both mock data files and React Query hooks; [ ] No any types used; [ ] Reviewed and agreed by both FE and BFF engineers before Sprint 2 begins

Summary: Mock data alignment with API contract
Description: Update all frontend mock data files (src/mocks/*.ts) to conform exactly to the interfaces defined in BFF-019 (src/types/api.ts). Ensures that switching from mock to real BFF requires only a base URL change and zero type fixes.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] All mock data files import and satisfy types from src/types/api.ts; [ ] TypeScript compiler passes with no errors on mock data; [ ] Mock response shapes match real API contract field-for-field; [ ] No fields present in mock that are absent from interface definitions

Description: Define Pydantic v2 models for all BFF endpoint responses, mirroring the TypeScript interfaces in BFF-019. Use model_config for JSON serialization settings (alias, populate_by_name). Attach response_model to all FastAPI route decorators. Verify OpenAPI docs auto-generated at /docs reflect correct schemas.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Pydantic models defined for all endpoint responses; [ ] Field names and types match TypeScript interfaces in BFF-019; [ ] All route decorators have response_model set; [ ] /docs shows correct request/response schemas; [ ] snake_case Python fields serialize to camelCase JSON for frontend consumption

Description: Implement GET /api/companies/search?q=&limit=&offset=. Query the companies table for records where name LIKE %q%. Return id, name, industry, sub_industry, location_city, location_state, logo_url. Support pagination via limit (default 10, max 50) and offset. Results ordered by name ascending. Unblocks FE-007.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Returns matching companies for partial name search (case-insensitive); [ ] Pagination works correctly with limit and offset; [ ] Empty query string returns empty results (no full table scan); [ ] Response conforms to CompanySearchResult[] interface; [ ] SQL injection safe (parameterized query); [ ] Responds in under 500ms for typical queries

Description: Implement three endpoints for user favorites. GET /api/companies/favorites returns current user's favorited companies with all fields needed for CompanyCard (name, industry, location, risk flag count, follow-up question count, updated_at, pinned). POST /api/favorites/:company_id adds a favorite. DELETE /api/favorites/:company_id removes a favorite. user_id sourced from auth middleware. Unblocks FE-009 and FE-011.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] GET returns favorited companies for authenticated user only; [ ] All CompanyCard fields included in response; [ ] POST creates favorite record; returns 409 if already favorited; [ ] DELETE removes favorite; returns 404 if not found; [ ] All operations use user_id from request.state.user; [ ] Response conforms to FavoriteCompany[] interface

Description: Implement GET /api/companies/recent returning the authenticated user's 30-day viewing history as a list with fields for table display (name, industry, location, risk_flag_count, follow_up_question_count, viewed_at). Implement POST /api/companies/recent/:company_id to record a view event — this also writes to user_activity table (event_type: view_company). Return at most 50 records ordered by viewed_at descending. Unblocks FE-010.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] GET returns viewing history for last 30 days; [ ] Results ordered by viewed_at descending; [ ] Maximum 50 records returned; [ ] POST inserts into user_recent_views and user_activity atomically; [ ] Duplicate views within 1 hour are deduplicated (upsert on company_id + user_id with updated_at); [ ] Response conforms to RecentCompany[] interface

Description: Implement POST /api/activity. Accepts event_type (view_company / click_risk_flags / click_followup / update_metrics), company_id, metadata (JSON), and timestamp. Writes to user_activity table asynchronously using FastAPI BackgroundTasks so the response returns immediately without waiting for the DB write. Unblocks FE-027.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Endpoint returns 202 Accepted immediately; [ ] DB write happens in background task; [ ] Accepts all four event_type values; [ ] metadata stored as JSON in user_activity.metadata column; [ ] Invalid event_type returns 400; [ ] user_id sourced from auth token, not request body

Description: Implement GET /api/users/preferences/:company_id returning the user's saved metric selections for a given company. Implement PUT /api/users/preferences/:company_id accepting selected_metrics as a JSON array of metric names and upserting into user_metric_preferences. Unblocks FE-023 and FE-019.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] GET returns selected_metrics array for user + company combination; [ ] GET returns empty array (not 404) if no preferences saved yet; [ ] PUT upserts correctly (insert on first save, update on subsequent); [ ] selected_metrics stored and returned as string array; [ ] Response conforms to UserPreferences interface

Description: Implement GET /api/companies/:id returning full company profile: id, name, industry, sub_industry, location_city, location_state, logo_url, updated_at, plus is_favorited boolean for the authenticated user. Unblocks FE-012.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Returns correct company data for given id; [ ] Returns 404 if company not found; [ ] is_favorited reflects current user's favorites; [ ] updated_at reflects latest batch_date; [ ] Response conforms to CompanyProfile interface

Description: Implement GET /api/companies/:id/insights. Returns the 6 most recent insight cards from company_insights table filtered by company_id and latest batch_date. Each insight includes: insight_type, severity (red/yellow/green), title, summary_text. Unblocks FE-013.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Returns exactly 6 insights (or fewer if less exist); [ ] Filtered to latest batch_date; [ ] severity values are red / yellow / green only; [ ] Returns 404 if company not found; [ ] Response conforms to CompanyInsight[] interface

Description: Implement GET /api/companies/:id/metrics?category= returning metrics from company_metrics table. Optional category filter (capital / profitability / credit_risk / exposure). Returns metric_name, metric_value, metric_unit, period_date for the latest batch_date. Unblocks FE-014 and FE-021.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Returns metrics for specified category; [ ] Returns all categories if category param omitted; [ ] Filtered to latest batch_date; [ ] Response includes metric_name / metric_value / metric_unit / period_date; [ ] Returns empty array (not 404) if no metrics for category; [ ] Response conforms to CompanyMetric[] interface

Description: Implement GET /api/companies/:id/trends?name= returning time series data from company_performance_trends. Optional name filter for trend_name. Response format: array of {name, data: [[date_string, value], ...]} ready for direct ECharts consumption. Unblocks FE-020.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Returns trend data in [[date, value], ...] format per series; [ ] Dates formatted as ISO date strings (YYYY-MM-DD); [ ] Optional name filter works correctly; [ ] Returns all trends if name param omitted; [ ] Data ordered by period_date ascending; [ ] Response conforms to PerformanceTrend[] interface

Description: Implement GET /api/companies/:id/loan-portfolio returning rows from company_loan_portfolio for the latest period_date. Fields: loan_type, balance, pct_of_total, yoy_growth, npl_ratio. Include a totals row. Unblocks FE-015.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Returns all loan type rows for latest period_date; [ ] Totals row included in response with loan_type: 'Total'; [ ] yoy_growth returned as decimal (e.g. 0.048 for 4.8%); [ ] Returns empty array if no data; [ ] Response conforms to LoanPortfolioRow[] interface

Description: Implement GET /api/companies/:id/risk-insights?category= returning records from company_risk_insights. Optional category filter (risk_legal / event_signals / qualitative / governance). Each record includes: category, severity, title, body_text, source_date. Filtered to latest batch_date. Unblocks FE-016.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Returns risk insights for specified category; [ ] Returns all categories if category param omitted; [ ] Filtered to latest batch_date; [ ] severity values are high / medium / low; [ ] Response conforms to RiskInsight[] interface

Description: Implement GET /api/companies/:id/activity returning events from company_activity ordered by event_date descending (max 20). Implement GET /api/companies/:id/follow-up-questions?category= returning questions from company_followup_questions with optional category filter. Both filtered to latest batch_date. Unblocks FE-017 and FE-018.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Activity returns max 20 events ordered by event_date desc; [ ] Each activity includes activity_type, icon_color, title, summary_text, event_date; [ ] Follow-up questions support category filter; [ ] Both endpoints return empty arrays (not 404) when no data; [ ] Responses conform to CompanyActivity[] and FollowUpQuestion[] interfaces

Description: Implement POST /api/companies/:id/export-email. Aggregate company profile, key insights, risk insights, and follow-up questions into a structured HTML email template. Send via Azure Communication Services Email SDK (or fallback SMTP). Email sent to authenticated user's email address (from token). Return 202 on successful dispatch.
Acceptance Criteria: [ ] Email assembled with company name, key insights summary, top risk insights, and follow-up questions; [ ] HTML email renders correctly in Outlook and Gmail; [ ] Sent to user email from Azure AD token; [ ] Returns 202 Accepted on successful send; [ ] Returns 500 with error envelope if send fails; [ ] No sensitive data (raw SQL values, internal IDs) exposed in email body
